2606.25627.title: TL++: Accuracy and Privacy Preserving Traversal Learning for Distributed Intelligent Systems
2606.25627.paper_summary: Distributed intelligent systems increasingly need to train across data silos without centralizing raw data. Federated learning keeps data local but can suffer under heterogeneous partitions and requires repeated full-model exchange. Split learning reduces communication through cut-layer activations, but standard protocols generally do not recover centralized mini-batch gradient behavior and may expose activations and gradients in plaintext. We present TL++, a two-mode traversal-learning framework that constructs virtual batches across nodes to recover centralized mini-batch gradient behavior under explicit synchronization assumptions. Base mode exchanges cut-layer activations and gradients rather than full models. Secure mode secret-shares each cut-layer activation and gradient between an orchestrator and a non-colluding helper, preventing either server from observing plaintext cut-layer tensors. This protection is limited to a semi-honest two-server setting; labels and loss-related outputs remain visible to the orchestrator. In the lightweight secure path evaluated here, exactness requires a linear or affine server path, while nonlinear operations require nonlinear MPC or approximation. We formalize TL++, analyze communication and computation costs, and evaluate it against federated and split-learning baselines on CIFAR-10 and BioGPT/PubMedQA using full fine-tuning and LoRA. On CIFAR-10, TL++ base cut 1 and exact secure cut 3 achieve accuracies of 91.41% (SD 0.19) and 90.93% (SD 0.17), respectively, exceeding the strongest measured non-TL++ baseline by more than 12 percentage points. TL++ base cut 1 also reduces per-step communication by 13.1-fold relative to full-model synchronization. PubMedQA results similarly favor TL++. Overall, TL++ approaches centralized-training performance while reducing communication and providing activation-level secret sharing.
2606.25627.paper_summary_zh: 分散式智能系統越來越需要在數據孤島之間進行訓練，而無需集中原始數據。聯邦學習保持數據本地，但在異質分區下可能會遭遇困難，並且需要重複的全模型交換。分割學習通過切層激活減少通信，但標準協議通常無法恢復集中式小批量梯度行為，並且可能會以明文形式暴露激活和梯度。我們提出了TL++，一種雙模式遍歷學習框架，通過在節點之間構建虛擬批次來恢復在明確同步假設下的集中式小批量梯度行為。基本模式交換切層激活和梯度，而不是全模型。安全模式在協調者和不合謀的幫助者之間秘密共享每個切層激活和梯度，防止任何一個伺服器觀察明文切層張量。這種保護僅限於半誠實的雙伺服器設置；標籤和損失相關輸出仍然對協調者可見。在這裡評估的輕量級安全路徑中，精確性需要線性或仿射伺服器路徑，而非線性操作則需要非線性MPC或近似。我們對TL++進行了形式化，分析了通信和計算成本，並在CIFAR-10和BioGPT/PubMedQA上對其進行了與聯邦和分割學習基準的評估，使用全微調和LoRA。在CIFAR-10上，TL++基本切割1和精確安全切割3的準確率分別達到91.41%（標準差0.19）和90.93%（標準差0.17），超過了最強的非TL++基準超過12個百分點。TL++基本切割1還將每步通信減少了相對於全模型同步的13.1倍。PubMedQA的結果同樣偏向於TL++。總體而言，TL++接近集中訓練性能，同時減少通信並提供激活級別的秘密共享。
2606.25627.author: Erdenebileg Batbaatar et.al.
